methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Andrenam is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Matej Cernosek and Alex Chu that has raised $10M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 private companies and 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) operating in the same sector at similar or adjacent funding stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $10M total raised as a valuation proxy, applying a 2.5x multiplier typical for Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $25M. Peer-median analysis examines four same-sector comparables spanning adjacent stages: Tenna Systems (Seed, $60M), Ares Industries (Series A, $150M), Vatn Systems (Series A, $300M), and Stark Defence (Series A, $500M). The median valuation across this set is $225M, with a range of $60M–$500M. These two methods triangulate to a low of $21M, median of $125M, and high of $259M.

The wide dispersion between methods reflects material uncertainty regarding Andrenam's relative competitive positioning and revenue trajectory within the Defense & Security sector. The peer set includes both Seed and Series A companies, introducing stage-related variance. Reconciliation toward a defensible point estimate would require additional data on Andrenam's revenue, customer traction, or technology differentiation relative to the identified comparables.
